pr.title: src/melee/gr/*: Rename lbl_ -> func_ for all functions
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Mechanical naming cleanup across the melee/gr stage code: functions that were still named with lbl_ address labels were renamed to func_ address names, and all C, header, function-pointer table, inline-asm relocation, and one external asm data-table reference were updated consistently. The PR had no body or review discussion, so the rationale is inferred from the title and diff: distinguish code symbols from data/local labels and standardize decomp placeholder naming for actual functions.
what_changed: Renamed many address-only function symbols in src/melee/gr from lbl_XXXXXXXX to func_XXXXXXXX without changing behavior.
Updated StageCallbacks arrays and StageData initializers in grbattle.c, grshrine.c, and grstory.c so callback function pointers use the new func_ names.
Updated matching function declarations in grbattle.h, grshrine.h, grstory.h, ground.h, and grlib.h to keep prototypes synchronized with C definitions.
Updated ground.c function-pointer uses passed to GObj_InitUserData, func_8038FD54, and func_801C10B8 from lbl_ names to func_ names.

Updated inline assembly symbol references in ground.c, including @ha/@l relocations such as lbl_801C0478@ha/@l to func_801C0478@ha/@l and scheduler callback references such as lbl_801C1CD0/lbl_801C1D38 to func_801C1CD0/func_801C1D38.
Updated asm/melee/if/ifmagnify.s .4byte table entries to point to renamed ground functions such as func_801C0690, func_801C0654, func_801C06A4, func_801C0668, and func_801C067C.
Touched 11 files with balanced added/deleted lines, indicating a pure rename/symbol-reference cleanup rather than logic or matching changes.
smart_moves: The rename was propagated through all relevant reference forms, not just C definitions: prototypes, stage callback tables, StageData callback slots, C callback registration calls, inline asm relocations, and external .s data-table entries were all updated together.
Keeping the address suffixes unchanged preserved symbol traceability while correcting the placeholder prefix from lbl_ to func_.
The diff was narrowly scoped and line-balanced, which made the rename low-risk and easy to review/merge quickly.
Updating asm/melee/if/ifmagnify.s avoided stale symbol references from non-C translation units that would otherwise break linkage or symbol consistency.
decomp_lessons: In this codebase, address-named placeholders for known functions should use func_XXXXXXXX, while lbl_XXXXXXXX should be reserved for labels/data/unknown labels rather than callable functions.
When renaming decomp symbols, search beyond the defining C file: callback tables, headers, inline asm @ha/@l references, and standalone assembly files can all hold symbol references.
Stage modules commonly expose many callbacks via StageCallbacks and StageData; these tables are high-priority places to update after function renames.
For partial decomp files containing inline asm, symbol renames must be mirrored inside assembly comments/instructions where operands name the old symbol.
A pure symbol cleanup can still be important decomp infrastructure work because it improves automated search, function inventory accuracy, and future semantic renaming passes.
naming_conventions: Use func_XXXXXXXX for functions whose semantic name is still unknown but whose entry address is known.
Do not leave callable functions named lbl_XXXXXXXX once they are identified as functions.
Preserve the hexadecimal address portion during placeholder renames to retain map/symbol traceability.
Keep C definitions and header prototypes using exactly the same placeholder symbol names.
Update callback table entries to the function-style prefix when table members point to functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For inline asm relocation operands, rename both high and low relocation references together, e.g. old_symbol@ha and old_symbol@l must both become new_symbol@ha and new_symbol@l.
For assembly data tables using .4byte function pointers, update entries to the renamed func_ symbols so assembly and C agree at link time.
Callback registration calls such as func_8038FD54 and GObj_InitUserData can reveal that a lbl_ symbol is actually a function and should receive a func_ placeholder name.
No evidence of codegen/matching changes beyond symbol names; added and deleted line counts are balanced across all files.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR context.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes were available; lessons are inferred from title and diff only.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining src/melee/gr functions still named lbl_ after PR 367.
Search all asm/**/*.s .4byte entries that reference lbl_ symbols known to be functions.
Audit StageCallbacks and StageData tables for callback entries still using lbl_ names.
Check whether headers in src/melee/gr declare static functions and whether later cleanup removed or reorganized those prototypes.
